assertions.expectation: Recommends increasing maxSurge significantly (e.g., 10-20 or higher) to parallelize node replacements
assertions.reason: Response shows specific examples with maxSurge values of 5, 8, and 10, demonstrating the concept of increasing surge for parallelization

assertions.expectation: Explains that higher maxSurge means more nodes upgrading simultaneously but requires more compute quota
assertions.reason: While maxSurge is discussed, there is no explanation about compute quota requirements or the relationship between higher surge and quota needs

assertions.expectation: Advises checking and requesting increased compute quota before the upgrade
assertions.reason: No mention of checking or requesting increased compute quota before upgrades

assertions.expectation: Suggests staggering node pool upgrades or running non-conflicting pools in parallel
assertions.reason: Response includes automated upgrade pipeline showing staggered upgrades and parallel execution of pool upgrades using async flags

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maxUnavailable can be set >0 for non-critical pools to speed things up further
assertions.reason: Response shows maxUnavailable set to 1 and 2 in examples, demonstrating the concept of allowing unavailable nodes to speed upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends expanding the maintenance window or splitting across multiple weekends
assertions.reason: No recommendation to expand maintenance window or split across multiple weekends; response focuses on fitting within existing 8-hour window

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for updating surge settings on each pool
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands provided showing how to update maxSurge and maxUnavailable settings for node pools

assertions.expectation: Includes monitoring commands to track upgrade progress in real-time
assertions.reason: Response includes monitoring script with gcloud container operations list and kubectl get nodes commands for tracking progress
